text: The laparoscopic exploration reveals multiple diverticula, each measuring up to three or four centimeters, along a segment of the jejunum approximately one hundred centimeters in length. The jejunal diverticular segment, located twenty centimeters distal to the right, is marked with a silk suture.
The surgeon marks the distal end of the jejunal segment to be resected with silk suture. Using a vessel sealer, the meso-oxygen is released from proximal to distal along the segment.

The surgeon uses ICG to confirm normal circulation at the distal end of the intestine before transecting it with a robotic stapler. The proximal end is similarly controlled with ICG and transected. A jejunal segment approximately one hundred centimeters long is resected and removed through a suprapubic incision. The intestinal ends are then closed with sutures.
The surgeon performs cetabular incisions and completes a side-to-side anastomosis using a robotic stapler. The cetabular incisions are then sutured using velar sutures.

Surgery was completed after bleeding control. Pathology showed regional diverticulosis with congenital diverticula. No postoperative complications were observed. Depression was charged in the fifth day.
